frameworks.name: sense of agency
frameworks.one_line: self-authorship of movement assessed via first-order and metacognitive judgments

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: discrimination between self-influenced vs random motion stimuli

core_question: Can a short online motion-tracking task reliably quantify movement agency through first-order perceptual and second-order metacognitive judgments?
key_assumptions: agency can be probed by distinguishing self-influenced from random trajectories
first-order perception and second-order metacognition are dissociable components of agency
manipulations: trajectory dependence on participant cursor movement vs random motion
task difficulty adapted online to participant performance
key_findings: task yields reliable first-order and metacognitive agency measures in healthy participants
online format is efficient and short compared to existing assessments
interpretations: the task is a promising tool for diagnosing and understanding disorders of agency
limitations_noted: validation limited to healthy participants in this report
